title: AI Research Intern (3D Motion Generation)
company: University of Alberta | Vision and Learning Lab
description:
Mitacs Globalink research excellence scholarship, at Prof. Li Cheng's Vision and Learning Lab. Together with Yilin Wang, I work on a model for music- and text-prompt-conditioned multi-person 3D dance generation, aiming for a CVPR submission.

My first step is to make the data reliable, meaning physically correct motion. In practice, I estimate the ground plane robustly and fix motion artifacts (foot-skating, floating, dancer-to-dancer interpenetration, foot-ground contacts), while building the physics-based evaluation metrics, on the AIOZ-GDANCE dataset. After that, I'll move on to the architecture of the generative model itself.
